fr:mainmatter.html:p: The minimal Cache coherence state machine that supports write-back caches. Each cache line is in one of three states:

html:ol.html:li: Invalid (I) — this cache does not hold a valid copy.
Shared (S) — clean copy, read permission, possibly held by other caches.
Modified (M) — only copy, exclusive read+write permission, dirty (must be written back on eviction).

html:ul.html:li: BusRdX — request exclusive ownership for a write.
BusInv — invalidate other caches on a write hit.
BusWB — write back a dirty block on eviction.
BusReply — supply the value to a requester instead of memory.

fr:mainmatter.html:div: Remark. S → M requires either a BusInv or BusRdX so that other sharers invalidate. The move from S to M is not silent.
Remark. MESI protocol adds a fourth state (Exclusive) to optimize the common case of thread-private data — a line read by exactly one core can be upgraded to writable without a bus transaction.

fr:mainmatter.html:p: MSI protocol augmented with an Exclusive state. The four states:

html:ol.html:li: Shared (S) — clean, possibly held by other caches, read-only.

fr:mainmatter.html:p: The crucial addition is that a line transitioning S → M needs a bus transaction (other sharers must invalidate), but a line transitioning E → M is silent: nobody else has a copy, so nobody needs to be told. Eviction from E also skips the writeback (E is clean), saving memory traffic.
MESI is dramatically better than MSI for thread-private data: a thread that reads then writes its private variable goes I → E → M with only one bus transaction, instead of I → S → M with two.
The next refinement is MOESI protocol, which adds an Owned state for shared-but-dirty lines.

In a Multicore system where every core has its own Cache hierarchy, the same memory address can live in multiple caches simultaneously. A cache coherence protocol is the mechanism that keeps those copies consistent — propagating writes, invalidating stale copies, and preserving two invariants:

html:ol.html:li: Single-writer invariant: at any moment, at most one core has permission to write a given line. No other core can read or write it concurrently.
Data-value invariant: all cores see the same order of updates to a given line.

html:ul.html:li: snoop-based coherence — every cache watches a shared bus and reacts to other cores' traffic. Scales to ~8 cores.
directory-based coherence — a structure tracks who has a copy of each line; coherence messages are point-to-point. Scales to hundreds of cores at the cost of an extra hop.

fr:mainmatter.html:p: The concrete state machines are protocols like MSI protocol, MESI protocol, MOESI protocol, and DEC Firefly, each adding states to handle more cases or to optimize specific sharing patterns. A worked implementation of directory-based MESI in Murphi lives in Implementing Directory-based MESI in Murphi.
Worked instance: Implementing Directory-based MESI in Murphi implements directory-based MESI in Murphi.
MSI protocol augmented with an Exclusive state. The four states:

html:ol.html:li: Shared (S) — clean, possibly held by other caches, read-only.

fr:mainmatter.html:p: The crucial addition is that a line transitioning S → M needs a bus transaction (other sharers must invalidate), but a line transitioning E → M is silent: nobody else has a copy, so nobody needs to be told. Eviction from E also skips the writeback (E is clean), saving memory traffic.
MESI is dramatically better than MSI for thread-private data: a thread that reads then writes its private variable goes I → E → M with only one bus transaction, instead of I → S → M with two.
The next refinement is MOESI protocol, which adds an Owned state for shared-but-dirty lines.

In a Multicore system where every core has its own Cache hierarchy, the same memory address can live in multiple caches simultaneously. A cache coherence protocol is the mechanism that keeps those copies consistent — propagating writes, invalidating stale copies, and preserving two invariants:

html:ol.html:li: Single-writer invariant: at any moment, at most one core has permission to write a given line. No other core can read or write it concurrently.
Data-value invariant: all cores see the same order of updates to a given line.

html:ul.html:li: snoop-based coherence — every cache watches a shared bus and reacts to other cores' traffic. Scales to ~8 cores.
directory-based coherence — a structure tracks who has a copy of each line; coherence messages are point-to-point. Scales to hundreds of cores at the cost of an extra hop.

fr:mainmatter.html:p: The concrete state machines are protocols like MSI protocol, MESI protocol, MOESI protocol, and DEC Firefly, each adding states to handle more cases or to optimize specific sharing patterns. A worked implementation of directory-based MESI in Murphi lives in Implementing Directory-based MESI in Murphi.
Worked instance: Implementing Directory-based MESI in Murphi implements directory-based MESI in Murphi.
